name: Holderschmarren (Elderberry Scramble)
recipeIngredient: 250 g Very ripe, pitted damson plums
150 g Rye bread or white French bread, crust removed,
50 g Butter (3 and a half tablespoons)
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt the butter in a pan over medium heat.
2. Add the sliced elderberries, pears, and damson plums to the pan. Cook for a few minutes until they soften slightly.
3. Make a thin paste by whisking together flour and milk (or buttermilk) in a small bowl.
4. Pour the flour mixture into the pan with the fruit and stir to combine.
5. Gradually add the remaining milk, stirring constantly to avoid lumps.
6. Add the sliced bread, allowing it to absorb some of the liquid.
7. Cook for another 2-3 minutes until the plums are soft and the bread is fully cooked through.
8. Season with salt, sugar, cinnamon, and lemon juice to taste.
9. Serve hot, garnished with additional fresh fruit if desired.
Serves 4.
